title: This German Man Is on a Quest to Cut the Perfect Slice of Bread
gist: Profile of 'Germanbreadcutter,' an anonymous German creator who has amassed over 100,000 followers across Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube since February by posting videos of himself slicing bread to sub-millimeter precision and tracking results in a self-made 'breadsheet.'

insights.claim: A single anonymous German creator, Germanbreadcutter, built a following of over 100,000 across Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube in roughly five months (since February) around one repetitive, hyper-niche skill: cutting bread slices with sub-millimeter thickness precision measured by digital caliper.
insights.speaker: Miles Klee
insights.so_what: Another data point for the 'hyper-specific niche skill content' pattern (ASMR, precision crafts) that continues to prove viral distribution doesn't require broad subject matter, relevant to understanding what still drives organic growth on saturated short-form platforms.
insights.quote: Since February, he has amassed more than 100,000 followers across Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube thanks to a single surprising skill: his ability to slice bread so evenly that you'd swear it wasn't cut by hand.

insights.claim: The creator promotes specific third-party tools he uses (Piklohas and Hoshanho bread knives, a Kynup digital caliper made by a Chinese manufacturer) via affiliate-style links, despite having no formal brand partnerships yet.
insights.speaker: Miles Klee
insights.so_what: Illustrates the early-stage monetization pipeline for niche micro-influencers — product links before formal sponsorship deals — a template smaller consumer brands can use to reach engaged niche audiences cheaply.
insights.locator: paragraph on his day job and tools
